title: DIY Tabletop Book Holder
summary: This tabletop book holder is a fun accent for any room, and can be customized to the length and color you choose.
description: This tabletop book holder is a fun accent for any room, and can be customized to the length and color you choose. It's a great beginner woodworking project!

steps.title: Cut Wood
steps.body: Cut wood to the listed dimensions.

steps.title: Sand
steps.body: Use a sander or sanding block to sand everything smooth.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill 2 pocket holes at each end of your 6 1/4" long 1x2 boards, and 3 pocket holes along each short end of your 1x10 board.

steps.title: Build Side Pieces
steps.body: Build your 2 side pieces by attaching 2 of your 6 1/4" long 1x2 boards to 2 of your 10" long 1x2 boards using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws, creating a rectangle. Repeat this step so you have 2 rectangular side pieces.

steps.title: Attach Bottom to Sides
steps.body: Attach your rectangular side pieces to your 18" long 1x10 board using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. I attached mine so that my screws would be on the bottom and therefore not visible.

steps.title: Add Feet
steps.body: Using wood glue and a brad nailer, attach your dowel pieces to the bottom of your bookshelf. I measured 1 inch in from the side edge and 1/2" in from the front/back edge and marked with a pencil before repositioning them to attach.

steps.title: Paint or Stain
steps.body: Paint or stain your bookshelf. I used a special walnut colored stain for this one.
